reply:
[[BLOCK:p_18]]
This syllabus is subject to change at the instructor’s discretion. Any revisions will be announced through Lumina.

[[BLOCK:p_20]]
Late arrivals: If a student arrives late for a quiz or exam, the instructor may, at their discretion, allow or deny the student permission to take the assessment. If permission is granted, the student will have only the remaining time to complete the quiz or exam.

[[BLOCK:p_22]]
Students who successfully complete this course will be able to:

[[BLOCK:p_23]]
Understand and critically evaluate the concept of epistemology (认识论), recognizing that knowledge is constructed through multiple systems for gathering, organizing, analyzing, and evaluating information, and that there are multiple pathways to solving problems.

[[BLOCK:p_24]]
Formulate research questions or identify problems; locate, collect, and organize relevant peer-reviewed (同行评审) research; draw evidence-based conclusions; apply appropriate problem-solving strategies; and communicate findings effectively to others.

[[BLOCK:p_25]]
Analyze the content, arguments, and rhetorical strategies of information sources; evaluate their credibility, reliability, and validity; and consider the influence of context.

[[BLOCK:p_26]]
Apply reasoning skills by identifying theoretical assumptions, considering multiple perspectives (including one’s own), making inferences, and drawing conclusions, while supporting arguments with logic and evidence.

[[BLOCK:p_27]]
Interpret numerical data and place it within the broader context of psychology concepts.

[[BLOCK:p_28]]
Recognize that numerical data can be interpreted in multiple ways and may be subject to manipulation, and identify methodological limitations, potential biases, and ethical implications in research.

[[BLOCK:p_29]]
Apply appropriate disciplinary methods and techniques to investigate research questions, generate predictions, test hypotheses, or address practical problems.

[[BLOCK:p_30]]
Understand the foundational assumptions of psychology and their relationship to its research paradigms, including how these assumptions shape theoretical development and the construction of meaning.
